notes: Open plate/subplate assembly on a shallow top-front shelf; keep one direct cutoff common feed into one side, five heavy output cables from the opposite side through an attached comb/gland strip, one enlarged double-wire output access hole, and do not mount it on the engine-side face.

notes: Folded aluminium base/guard at the most accessible top/front position; lips bend upward around the 100A breaker/terminal side. Route cutoff output directly to both covered relay and MIDI common feed. Open final mounting holes after measuring the real breaker and lug sweep.

notes: Vehicle-side widened raised ladder for the front stack; mounts relay outboard enough for cover service and provides top-front shelf/tab pickups, three relay wire-exit clearances, attached MIDI output comb/gland support, double-output access hole, cutoff splitter, and wire-gutter P-clips for MIDI, cutoff, direct relay/MIDI feeds, and five MIDI output cables.
Do not place relay, MIDI, or cutoff faces here by default. Use only protected cable clips/pass-throughs after steering, hose, heat, and service sweeps are proven.

notes: One 4 mm formed saddle goes over the chassis rail with legs down both sides and through-bolts at the known vehicle-side chassis location. May 14 no-battery bay photo suggests starting with 180 mm vertical rise from chassis top to tray underside.

notes: Use slotted upper bridge holes or removable spacers/packers to tune side position. Keep the battery in the existing pocket and keep the engine/LHD side clear for steering, hose, and service sweep.
